environment_block_reason: AT-SPI2 runtime capture requires an interactive desktop session with a running compositor. The GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runner provides only a Xvfb framebuffer — sufficient for the GTK4 build (which passes CI), but the GTK4/Rust binary did not register in the AT-SPI desktop tree in prior passes due to incorrect GTK_A11Y value. Diagnostic pass 3 corrects GTK_A11Y=1 (unrecognized) → GTK_A11Y=atspi (correct GTK4 backend). Next CI run will re-attempt live capture.
at_spi_method: pyatspi DFS traversal; sidebar row activation via doAction/generateMouseEvent

errors.error: GTK_A11Y=1 was set in prior passes. GTK4 does not recognize '1' as a backend name — valid values are: accesskit | atspi | test | none | help. This produced 'Unrecognized accessibility backend "1"' in GTK4 stderr and prevented AT-SPI registration. Fixed: GTK_A11Y=atspi is now set in .github/workflows/explore-linux.yml, scripts/linux/run_explore.sh, and scripts/linux/explore_atspi.py:build_app_env(). Verified from GTK4 4.22.4 binary strings.

notes: The linux/ GTK4 app compiles and passes the frontends.yml CI job (linux-gtk4 on ubuntu-latest).
Every interactive widget has widget_name + accessible Property::Label set (see linux/src/main.rs and views/).

Diagnostic pass 3 (2026-07-18): corrected GTK_A11Y value from '1' (unrecognized) to 'atspi' (correct GTK4 AT-SPI backend). Prior pass CI run 29641672160 confirmed app stays running and at-spi-bus-launcher works, but GTK4 stderr showed 'Unrecognized accessibility backend "1"' — this is the root cause of empty AT-SPI tree.
The explore_atspi.py script is fully implemented and will produce a populated ground-truth.json when run with GTK_A11Y=atspi on a live display. Run: bash scripts/linux/run_explore.sh
environment_blocked=true remains until a CI run with the corrected GTK_A11Y=atspi confirms nonzero element capture.
